description: Dockerfile builds a working image with Claude Code and aligned UID/GID
steps: Run docker build with default build-args
Verify build succeeds
Run docker build with NODE_VERSION=18 and a pinned CLAUDE_VERSION
Verify build succeeds with custom args
Run claude --version inside the built container
Verify claude binary responds
Create a file inside the container at /home/agent/workspace/
Verify the file is owned by the host user on the host filesystem
Run git, curl, jq, gh inside the container
Verify all four commands are available

description: Basic sandbox runs a prompt and returns Claude's response
steps: Run claude-sandbox with a simple prompt from a directory with a known file
Verify Claude's response appears on stdout
Verify the container no longer exists after the command exits
Attempt to write a file outside the workspace from within the sandbox
Verify the write fails (no host paths other than workspace are mounted)

description: --workspace flag mounts the specified directory instead of $PWD
steps: Run claude-sandbox with --workspace pointing to a known directory
Verify Claude sees the contents of that directory
Run claude-sandbox without --workspace
Verify Claude sees the contents of $PWD
Run claude-sandbox with --workspace pointing to a non-existent path
Verify exit code is 1 and error message names the missing path
Verify no Docker process was started for the missing path case

description: Unknown arguments are forwarded to claude verbatim
steps: Run claude-sandbox with --output-format stream-json
Verify output is JSONL
Run claude-sandbox with --model claude-haiku-4-5-20251001
Verify the specified model is used
Verify --workspace does not appear in the args received by claude
Verify --build does not appear in the args received by claude

description: Image is built automatically on first use and cached on subsequent runs
steps: Remove the target image if it exists
Run claude-sandbox with a prompt
Verify the image was built automatically before Claude ran
Run claude-sandbox again with a prompt
Verify no rebuild occurred on the second run
Run claude-sandbox --build
Verify the image is rebuilt
Verify the process exits without running Claude

description: Node version inside the sandbox matches the workspace .nvmrc
steps: Create a workspace with .nvmrc containing 18
Run claude-sandbox from that workspace
Verify the image tag is claude-sandbox:claudeX.Y.Z-node18
Run node --version inside the container
Verify it returns v18.x.x
Create a second workspace without .nvmrc
Run claude-sandbox from that workspace
Verify the image tag is claude-sandbox:claudeX.Y.Z-nodelts
Verify both images exist independently in docker image ls

description: Claude config and MCP servers from ~/.claude are available inside the sandbox
steps: Run claude-sandbox with a prompt that requires reading ~/.claude/settings.json
Verify Claude can access its settings
Run a prompt that invokes an MCP server configured in ~/.claude
Verify the MCP server responds correctly
After the session, check ~/.claude/projects/ on the host
Verify the sandbox session appears in the history

description: Missing ANTHROPIC_API_KEY produces a clear error before any Docker call
steps: Unset ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
Run claude-sandbox with a prompt
Verify the error message names ANTHROPIC_API_KEY explicitly
Verify exit code is 1
Verify no docker run process was started

description: Git works correctly inside the sandbox when the workspace is a worktree
steps: Create a git worktree from a test repo
Run claude-sandbox from the worktree
Run git status inside the sandbox
Verify git status succeeds and reflects the worktree state
Run git log inside the sandbox
Verify git log shows the repo history
Verify the parent .git mount is read-only
Run worktree detection logic as a pure unit test with a known gitdir line
Verify the correct parent git path is extracted
Run detection logic on a normal repo (directory .git)
Verify no parent git path is returned
